articles.title: Preoperative fall history is associated with increased postoperative complications at 2 years after primary total knee arthroplasty: A propensity-matched analysis.
articles.authors: Kang H, Ilyas MH, Freeman IA, Sampson WT, Mannina CM, Kwon YM
articles.population: Patients aged 65 years or over undergoing primary total knee arthroplasty
articles.outcomes: Postoperative falls, emergency department visits, readmissions, surgical site infections, medical complications, revision surgery, periprosthetic joint infection, periprosthetic fracture and mortality at 90 days, 1 year and 2 years
articles.take_home: Older patients who had fallen in the year before knee replacement surgery had more complications in the first 90 days after surgery and were more likely to fall again, need further surgery, develop infections, break bones around the implant or die within 2 years compared to similar patients without prior falls. Doctors should routinely ask about falls before surgery and provide extra support for patients at higher risk.

articles.title: Biomechanical phenotypes of 90 degrees change of direction in football players: Unsupervised machine learning in anterior cruciate ligament injury prevention.
articles.authors: Della Villa F, Ghibellini A, Buckthorpe M, Bononi L, Gabbrielli M, Grassi A, et al.
articles.study_type: Cohort study using unsupervised machine learning
articles.population: Healthy football players, mean age 16.3 years
articles.sample_size: 1,002 players performing 6,008 trials of a 90 degree change of direction task
articles.outcomes: Four distinct biomechanical phenotypes identified based on force production and movement control during change of direction movements
articles.take_home: The study found that young football players can be grouped into four different movement patterns when changing direction, ranging from poor movement control with low forces to good movement control with high forces. This artificial intelligence approach could help identify which players need specific injury prevention training to reduce ACL injury risk.

articles.title: A simple planning tool for tibial slope osteotomy-Osteotomy depth is a precise parameter to determine wedge height.
articles.authors: Vieider RP, Watrinet JM, Bilodeau R, Dadoo S, Vargas L, Mattar LT, et al.
articles.population: Patients with posterior tibial slope of 13 degrees or more undergoing anterior closing wedge high tibial osteotomy
articles.outcomes: Planning precision of wedge height calculation methods measured by correction error within plus or minus 1 degree threshold
articles.take_home: A new planning method using osteotomy depth and correction angle was more accurate than fixed ratio methods for calculating wedge height in tibial slope surgery, achieving the target correction in 83% of cases compared to 50% and 9% for the other methods.

articles.title: Alarmingly High Rates of Complications and Refracture Among Patients with Early Periprosthetic Femoral Fracture Within 30 Days After THA.
articles.authors: Acuna AJ, Forlenza EM, Jones CM, DeBenedetti A, Terhune EB, Della Valle CJ
articles.study_type: Retrospective cohort study using national database
articles.population: Patients who underwent primary total hip replacement between 2010 and 2022
articles.sample_size: Early fracture group matched to 1,725 patients with fracture at 31-90 days, 1,887 patients with fracture at 91 days-1 year, and 4,465 patients with no fracture within 1 year
articles.outcomes: Infection, refracture, and reoperation rates at 90 days and 2 years; complications within 90 days
articles.take_home: Patients who broke their hip bone near the implant within 30 days of hip replacement surgery had much higher rates of infection, another fracture, and need for further surgery over 2 years compared to those who fractured later or did not fracture. By 2 years, only 57% of early fracture patients were free from another fracture, compared to over 99% of patients without fractures.

articles.title: Minimal and Substantial Improvement Thresholds for Oxford Scores Following Primary Hip and Knee Replacement Based on Patient Satisfaction.
articles.authors: Valsamis EM, Dufresne M, Chambers K, Lapner P, Beaule P, Grammatopoulos G
articles.study_type: Prognostic study using logistic regression analysis
articles.population: Patients undergoing primary total hip or knee replacement for osteoarthritis
articles.outcomes: Oxford Hip Score and Oxford Knee Score thresholds that predict patient satisfaction; minimal clinically important difference and substantial clinical benefit scores identified
articles.take_home: The study identified specific Oxford Score thresholds that predict whether patients will be satisfied after hip and knee replacement surgery. These thresholds could help the NHS set targets for successful surgery outcomes, though patients with worse function before surgery may need higher improvement scores to feel satisfied.

articles.title: The Variable Axis Knee Prosthesis: A Legacy of Dr. David G. Murray.
articles.authors: Shaw JA
articles.population: Patients receiving knee prostheses
articles.outcomes: Clinical success of the Variable Axis knee prosthesis design over more than a decade of use
articles.take_home: Dr. David G. Murray's Variable Axis knee prosthesis, first used in patients in 1974, was the first unconstrained cruciate-substituting knee replacement and introduced metal-backed tibial components with modular polyethylene inserts that became standard in modern knee replacements. Although this design worked well clinically for over ten years, it was eventually replaced by more anatomically designed knee prostheses.

articles.title: Risk factors for manipulation under anesthesia after total knee arthroplasty and subsequent revision arthroplasty: a Finnish register-based study of 154,883 patients.
articles.authors: Sala J, Sirola J, Jaroma A, Kroger H, Sund R
articles.population: Patients aged 40 and over who had primary total knee replacement in Finland between 1999 and 2020
articles.sample_size: 154,883 patients, of which 3,861 required manipulation under anaesthesia
articles.outcomes: Risk factors for manipulation under anaesthesia within 1 year of primary knee replacement and subsequent revision knee replacement
articles.take_home: About 2.5% of patients needed manipulation under anaesthesia within a year of knee replacement. Women, younger patients, and those with diabetes, heart disease or high cholesterol were more likely to need this procedure. Patients who had manipulation were significantly more likely to need further surgery later, with 15% requiring revision within 10 years.

articles.title: Association between intraoperative fluoroscopy for anterior approach total hip arthroplasty and short-term revision risk: an analysis based on 49,878 cases from the Dutch Arthroplasty Register.
articles.authors: Vink MC, Zijlstra WP, Van Steenbergen LN, Van Veen PA, Doorn PF, Kuiper JWP, et al.
articles.population: Patients undergoing primary total hip arthroplasty via direct anterior approach in the Netherlands
articles.outcomes: Short-term revision risk at 6 months, 1, 2, and 2.5 years post-operatively
articles.take_home: Using fluoroscopy during hip replacement surgery via the anterior approach was associated with a slightly lower revision risk compared to surgery without fluoroscopy, though revision rates were low in both groups and the practical difference between the two approaches was small.

articles.title: Predicting persistent pain after total knee arthroplasty using different machine learning algorithms.
articles.authors: Rajamaki A, Reito A, Karsikas M, Niemelainen M, Eskelinen A
articles.study_type: Retrospective cohort study using machine learning
articles.population: Patients who underwent primary total knee arthroplasty
articles.outcomes: Persistent pain at 1 year after surgery, low Oxford Knee Score, and failure to achieve minimally clinical important difference in Oxford Knee Score
articles.take_home: Despite using advanced computer learning methods and analysing over 750 patient variables, the researchers found it very difficult to predict which patients would have ongoing pain after knee replacement surgery. The prediction model performed poorly, suggesting that pain after knee replacement is complex and hard to forecast even with large amounts of data.

articles.title: Association between spasticity of the hip and development of hip displacement in children: a cohort study of 786 hips.
articles.authors: Bakhtiyar M, Iljazi A, Odgaard A, Wong C, Petersen MM, Balslev-Clausen A
articles.population: Non-ambulant children with cerebral palsy (GMFCS levels IV-V)
articles.outcomes: Hip displacement (migration percentage ≥30%) and association with hip spasticity measured by Modified Ashworth Scale
articles.take_home: Children with cerebral palsy who have higher muscle tightness (spasticity) around the hip, especially in the inner thigh muscles, are more likely to develop hip displacement. The risk increased substantially as spasticity became more severe.

articles.title: Registration and reporting characteristics of trials investigating exercise therapy following total knee arthroplasty: a systematic review.
articles.authors: Gronfeldt BM, Husted RS, Brodsgaard RH, Holst L, Kallemose T, Chapple CM, et al.
articles.population: People undergoing total knee replacement for osteoarthritis
articles.outcomes: Effects of exercise therapy on knee function and pain; trial registration status and reporting quality
articles.take_home: Trials that were registered before starting showed smaller, more realistic benefits from exercise after knee replacement and were conducted with better quality standards than unregistered trials. Most trials did not clearly state their main outcome in advance, which can lead to misleading results.

articles.title: Mid- to long-term outcomes of short stem total hip arthroplasty in patients younger than 55 years: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Elbanna M, Vignaraja V, Kyriakides J, Tsamados S, Lewis TL, Bansal M, et al.
articles.population: Patients younger than 55 years undergoing short-stem total hip arthroplasty
articles.outcomes: Harris Hip Score improved from 45.3 to 93.8; complication rates: intraoperative fracture 1.6%, dislocation 0.6%, infection 0.4%; stem survivorship free from aseptic loosening 99.7%; overall revision rate 1.6%; radiological changes including osteolysis 1.3%, heterotopic ossification 1.9%, stem subsidence ⩾2 mm 0.7%
articles.take_home: Short-stem hip replacements in younger patients (under 55 years) show excellent results at mid- to long-term follow-up, with high implant survival rates and low complication rates. The authors emphasise these findings need confirmation in larger randomised studies before drawing firm conclusions.

articles.title: Cement Viscosity Is Associated With Aseptic Loosening After Primary Total Knee Arthroplasty: An Analysis of the American Joint Replacement Registry.
articles.authors: Harris A, Donnelly PC, Jennings JM, Martin JR, Khanuja HS, Hegde V
articles.population: Patients aged 65 years and older undergoing primary cemented total knee replacement
articles.outcomes: Revision surgery for aseptic loosening and periprosthetic joint infection
articles.take_home: Medium-viscosity cement was associated with a higher risk of the implant becoming loose over time compared with low or high-viscosity cement. Adding antibiotics to the cement did not reduce the risk of loosening or infection.

articles.title: Factors Associated with Patient-Reported Outcome Measure Non-Completion One Year after Total Joint Arthroplasty: A Retrospective Cohort Study.
articles.authors: Driessche AM, Zamzam M, Hodson N, Keinath C, Rubinfeld I, Charters M
articles.population: Adults who underwent primary total hip or knee replacement at a single health system between 2021 and 2023
articles.outcomes: Factors associated with failure to complete patient-reported outcome measures at one year after surgery, including patient portal use, preoperative opioid use, and neighbourhood socioeconomic vulnerability
articles.take_home: Not using the patient portal before surgery and taking opioids before surgery were the strongest predictors of patients not completing satisfaction surveys one year after hip or knee replacement. Patients in more deprived areas were also less likely to complete surveys after knee replacement.

articles.title: The EQ-5D-3L Questionnaire Demonstrates Moderate Ceiling Effects at One and Two Years After Total Hip Arthroplasty: Preoperative Predictors and Clinical Implications.
articles.authors: Lee YJ, Clement ND, Asopa V, Sochart DH
articles.population: Patients undergoing primary total hip arthroplasty
articles.outcomes: EQ-5D-3L quality of life scores at one and two years post-surgery; Oxford Hip Score; identification of ceiling effects and preoperative predictors
articles.take_home: About half of hip replacement patients reached the highest possible score on the EQ-5D-3L questionnaire by one year after surgery, limiting how much improvement could be measured in these patients. Patients with better quality of life before surgery were more likely to reach this ceiling, but those who did reach it still showed significant improvements in their hip function and wellbeing compared to before their operation.

articles.title: Neutrophil-Lymphocyte Ratio Trajectories Predict Mortality and Medical Complications in Hip Fracture Patients Undergoing Hemiarthroplasty.
articles.authors: Wier J, Jones IA, Kumaran P, Telang SS, Lieberman JR, Patterson JT, et al.
articles.study_type: Observational cohort study using national database
articles.population: Patients aged 55 years and older who had hip replacement surgery for a specific type of hip fracture
articles.outcomes: Mortality and medical complications following hip replacement surgery, stratified by inflammatory response patterns
articles.take_home: Patients whose inflammatory markers do not return to normal quickly after hip fracture surgery have higher risks of death and medical complications. Those with the slowest or most prolonged inflammatory response had the worst outcomes, with mortality rates ranging from 3.4% to 15.3% depending on their inflammatory pattern.

articles.title: Time-Dependent Revision Risk and Failure Modes After Total Hip Arthroplasty and Hemiarthroplasty for Displaced Femoral Neck Fracture in Patients Younger Than 70 Years: A Multicenter Analysis of 1,176 Consecutive Cases.
articles.authors: Won SH, Lee SH, Bahk JH, Jo WL, Lim YW, Huddleston JI 3rd, et al.
articles.population: Patients younger than 70 years with displaced femoral neck fracture treated with total hip arthroplasty or hemiarthroplasty
articles.outcomes: Cumulative incidence of revision surgery, modes of failure, mortality, infection rates, periprosthetic fracture rates, dislocation rates, and readmission rates
articles.take_home: Both procedures had similar revision rates up to 5.5 years, but total hip arthroplasty showed better long-term outcomes after this point. Hemiarthroplasty patients need closer follow-up after five years due to increased risk of bone loss and fractures.

articles.title: Internet Promotion of Orthobiologics by the American Association of Hip and Knee Surgeons Members: Claims Versus Evidence in Patient-Facing Websites.
articles.authors: Bellissimo J, Hall B, Hali K, Legler J, Khoshbin A, Atrey A
articles.population: Members of the American Association of Hip and Knee Surgeons across North America
articles.sample_size: 4,683 qualified websites associated with AAHKS members; 575 websites mentioning orthobiologics
articles.outcomes: Frequency of orthobiologic mentions on surgeon websites, types of benefit claims made, disclosure of risks, comparison of claims to peer-reviewed evidence
articles.take_home: Orthobiologic treatments like platelet-rich plasma and stem cell injections are promoted on about 12% of orthopaedic surgeon websites, but these sites often highlight benefits such as reduced pain without adequately discussing potential risks or acknowledging that the evidence for these treatments remains uncertain.

articles.title: Pain Catastrophizing Improves After Total Knee Arthroplasty, But Still Has a Negative Effect on Outcomes in Japanese Patients.
articles.authors: Kubo T, Kinoshita T, Kutsuna T, Watamori K, Hino K, Takao M
articles.study_type: Prospective cohort study with longitudinal follow-up
articles.population: Japanese patients undergoing total knee arthroplasty
articles.outcomes: Pain Catastrophizing Scale (PCS), Knee Society Score (KSS) satisfaction, and visual analogue scale (VAS) for pain measured preoperatively and at 7 days, 6 weeks, 6 months, and 1 year after surgery
articles.take_home: Although catastrophic thinking about pain improved after knee replacement surgery, patients who remained more catastrophic at 6 months were still less satisfied with their results at one year, even when accounting for their actual pain levels. This suggests that how patients think about their pain matters for satisfaction after surgery, not just the pain itself.

articles.title: The AAHKS Clinical Research Award: Maximizing Bearing Size Markedly Reduces Dislocations in Primary Total Hip Arthroplasty.
articles.authors: Wang E, McCormick K, Di Gangi C, Di Pauli von Treuheim T, Meftah M, Schwarzkopf R, et al.
articles.population: Patients undergoing primary fixed-bearing total hip arthroplasty
articles.sample_size: 8,607 patients (835 with maximised bearing diameter, 7,309 with second-largest bearing)
articles.outcomes: Dislocation rate; association between bearing diameter size and dislocation risk
articles.take_home: Using the largest available hip replacement bearing size for the socket markedly reduced dislocations, with no dislocations occurring in the maximised bearing group compared to 0.9% overall. This protective effect was substantially larger than other surgical factors that reduce dislocation risk.

articles.title: Axial Plane Alignment of the Knee (APAK): A Bony-Based Framework for Describing Flexion-Plane Knee Phenotypes and Its Association With Intraoperative Flexion-Gap Patterns.
articles.authors: Yu M, Hu M, Liu T, Xu H, Weng X, Feng B
articles.study_type: Radiological analysis with intraoperative robot-assisted cohort study
articles.population: Healthy knees, knees with osteoarthritis, and patients undergoing total knee replacement
articles.sample_size: 500 healthy knees, 500 osteoarthritic knees, and 180 robot-assisted total knee replacements
articles.outcomes: Association between APAK phenotype classification and intraoperative flexion-gap patterns; medial-lateral gap differences with mechanical versus functional alignment
articles.take_home: The APAK classification system reliably describes how the knee is aligned when bent to 90 degrees and significantly predicts the balance patterns surgeons find during knee replacement surgery. Using functional alignment rather than mechanical alignment reduced gap imbalances across different knee types.

articles.title: Tibia-first, gap-balanced patient-specific alignment in total knee arthroplasty achieves better ligament balance than mechanical, kinematic and functional alignment strategies.
articles.authors: Wierer G, Clatworthy M
articles.population: Patients undergoing primary robotic-assisted cruciate-retaining total knee replacement
articles.sample_size: 400 patients (188 women, 212 men, mean age 67 years)
articles.outcomes: Proportion of knees achieving balanced alignment (within +/- 2 mm) using four different alignment techniques: mechanical alignment, kinematic alignment, functional alignment, and patient-specific alignment
articles.take_home: Patient-specific alignment, particularly using a tibia-first, gap-balanced approach, achieved optimal balance in 98% of knee replacements, which was significantly better than the other three alignment techniques tested. This suggests that personalised alignment strategies may lead to better outcomes than standard techniques.

articles.title: Sustained long-term functional and patient-reported outcomes after complete proximal hamstring repair.
articles.authors: Esser KL, Powers I, Gosnell GG, Refuerzo J, Mercer NP, Gould HP, et al.
articles.population: Patients who underwent surgical repair of complete proximal hamstring tendon avulsions
articles.sample_size: 35 patients for patient-reported outcomes, 17 for physical examination at minimum 5-year follow-up
articles.outcomes: Functional outcomes including strength recovery (isokinetic knee flexion torque), thigh circumference, hip range of motion, patient-reported outcome measures (iHOT-12, LEFS, Marx Activity Scales, pain scores), return to sport rate, complications and reoperations
articles.take_home: Surgical repair of complete proximal hamstring tendon avulsions produces good long-term results at 7.6 years, with most patients (78%) returning to sport, minimal strength loss and high satisfaction, though 11% required repeat surgery.

articles.title: Biologically enhanced anterior cruciate ligament reconstruction: Platelet-rich plasma or bone marrow aspirate concentrate does not lead to better clinical or radiological outcomes-A randomized controlled trial of 175 cases with 2 years of follow-up.
articles.authors: Mokhtar AA, Gawish H, Abdelgawad A, Deghady A, Hassaan E, Matarawy K, et al.
articles.population: Patients undergoing anterior cruciate ligament reconstruction with semitendinosus quadruple autograft
articles.sample_size: 175 patients (control 79, platelet-rich plasma 53, bone marrow aspirate concentrate 43)
articles.outcomes: Clinical examination, Lysholm score, International Knee Documentation Committee score, Marx activity return to sport scale, and MRI signal intensity of ACL graft at 2 years
articles.take_home: Adding platelet-rich plasma or bone marrow aspirate concentrate to anterior cruciate ligament reconstruction did not improve patient outcomes or graft appearance on MRI compared to standard surgery alone. Bone marrow aspirate concentrate may have actually resulted in poorer graft appearance on imaging.

articles.title: Posterolateral corner reconstruction using hamstrings grafts-A comparative biomechanical study of three techniques.
articles.authors: Mestriner MB, Nejima S, Bartolin PB, de Paula Leite Cury R, Severino NR, Amis AA
articles.study_type: Biomechanical laboratory study using cadaveric knees
articles.population: Cadaveric knee specimens
articles.outcomes: Knee laxity measurements in varus rotation, external tibial rotation and posterior tibial translation across different degrees of knee flexion
articles.take_home: The modified LaPrade technique did not fully restore normal knee stability in extension and early flexion, whilst the modified Arciero and popliteofibular loop techniques were more effective overall but may have overconstrained the knee in deeper flexion. All three hamstring-based reconstruction techniques showed similar results for posterior tibial translation.

articles.title: Advanced systems for intraoperative cartilage evaluation and treatment demonstrate early feasibility and a shift towards integrating artificial intelligence: A scoping review.
articles.authors: Moews LD, Alfonsi SA, Musahl V, Hirschmann MT, Cole BJ, Chahla J, et al.
articles.study_type: Scoping review of AI and advanced technologies for intraoperative cartilage management
articles.population: Cadaveric, in-vivo, and clinical studies using AI-based or computer navigation systems for cartilage lesion diagnosis, mapping, or treatment
articles.outcomes: AI-based systems showed Dice coefficients of 0.68-0.90 for segmentation; navigation systems reduced angular errors in graft placement from >12 degrees to <4 degrees; hybrid systems decreased plug orientation error from 15.4 degrees to 6.5 degrees; 3D laser scanning achieved mean defect measurement error of 0.46 mm and reduced workflow time to under 4 minutes
articles.take_home: Advanced computer-assisted and AI-based technologies show promise for improving the accuracy and precision of cartilage surgery in early studies, but more clinical research is needed to confirm their safety and effectiveness in real operating theatres.

articles.title: Language-Concordant Preoperative Education is Associated with Reduced Readmission Rates After Total Knee Arthroplasty.
articles.authors: Jummie Akinwunmi O, Buchalter WH, Greene NE, Crockatt WK, Cooper HJ, Geller JA, et al.
articles.population: Patients undergoing total knee arthroplasty
articles.outcomes: 30- and 90-day readmission rates, pain scores at one year
articles.take_home: Spanish-speaking patients who received preoperative education in their own language had significantly lower readmission rates after knee replacement surgery compared to before this service was introduced. These patients also reported better pain control at one year.

articles.title: Rotating-Hinge Revision Total Knee Arthroplasty for Arthrofibrosis Improves Motion Without Increasing Complications: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.
articles.authors: Ruelos VCB, Jayarajan NA, Shi JL, Elias TJ, Wainwright JD, Horani K, et al.
articles.population: Patients undergoing revision knee replacement for stiffness or arthrofibrosis
articles.sample_size: 7 studies; 224 rotating-hinge revisions (4 comparative studies also included 108 non-hinged revisions)
articles.outcomes: Range of motion improvement; complication rates; reoperation rates
articles.take_home: Rotating-hinge knee implants used in revision surgery for stiffness improved movement by an average of 42 degrees, and worked better than standard constrained implants without causing more problems or needing more repeat operations.

articles.title: Impact of Intervention Timing on the Failure of Debridement, Antibiotics, and Implant Retention for Acute Postoperative Periprosthetic Joint Infections: A Contemporary United States Population-Level Analysis.
articles.authors: Zedan AM, Barber T, Bini SA, Diaz-Ledezma C
articles.study_type: Retrospective cohort study using national claims database
articles.population: Adults who had acute postoperative periprosthetic joint infection after primary hip or knee replacement and underwent debridement, antibiotics, and implant retention within 12 weeks
articles.outcomes: Treatment failure (any septic surgical intervention within two years) stratified by time to intervention in two-week intervals
articles.take_home: Early treatment of joint infections after hip or knee replacement (within about seven weeks) has similar success rates, but waiting longer than seven weeks significantly increases the risk of treatment failure. The study suggests that around six to seven weeks is a reasonable time window for this type of infection treatment.

articles.title: Comparable Survivorship and Patient-Reported Outcomes Following Unicompartmental Knee Arthroplasty in Obese and Non-Obese Patients: An Updated Systematic Review.
articles.authors: Cruickshank M, Son H, Khalik HA, Chalmers BP
articles.population: Patients undergoing unicompartmental knee arthroplasty, stratified by body mass index
articles.sample_size: 27 studies analysing 27,271 knees; mean age 65 years, mean BMI 30.7
articles.outcomes: Implant survivorship at short-term (less than 5 years), mid-term (5 to 10 years), and long-term (10 years or more) follow-up; reasons for revision surgery; patient-reported outcome measures
articles.take_home: Obese patients undergoing partial knee replacement have similar implant survival and meaningful functional improvement compared to non-obese patients, though morbidly obese patients may have a slightly higher rate of revision for progressive arthritis. Obesity should not automatically prevent someone from having this operation.

articles.title: Declining Medicaid Reimbursement for Total Hip and Knee Arthroplasty from 2014 to 2022: A Comparison of Expansion and Non-Expansion States.
articles.authors: Holle AM, Moore ML, Henderson AP, Verhey JT, Haglin JM, Deckey DG, et al.
articles.study_type: Retrospective analysis of insurance claims data
articles.population: Medicaid patients undergoing total knee arthroplasty and total hip arthroplasty
articles.outcomes: Inflation-adjusted physician reimbursement rates for TKA and THA from 2014 to 2022, compared between Medicaid expansion and non-expansion states
articles.take_home: Medicaid payments for knee and hip replacement surgery fell significantly between 2014 and 2022, with knee replacement fees dropping by 22% overall and hip replacement fees falling by 32%. The decline in payments was similar regardless of whether states had expanded Medicaid.

articles.title: Soft-Tissue Balance and Quadriceps Muscle Strength Are Associated with Patient-Reported Outcomes Following Total Knee Arthroplasty, Independent of Alignment Strategy: A Prospective Randomized Trial.
articles.authors: Ueyama H, Nakagawa S, Fukunaga K, Takemura S, Ohyama Y, Minoda Y, et al.
articles.population: Patients undergoing total knee replacement with medial pivot design
articles.outcomes: Knee Society Score 2011, quadriceps muscle strength, functional scores, radiographic alignment, soft-tissue balance
articles.take_home: Alignment strategy (kinematically aligned versus mechanically aligned) did not significantly affect patient satisfaction or function after knee replacement. Instead, good soft-tissue balance during surgery and maintaining quadriceps muscle strength were more important for better outcomes.

articles.title: Why Total Knees Fail and Re-Fail: An Analysis of 20,568 Revision Cases from the Swiss Implant Registry.
articles.authors: Elsheikh R, Amsler F, Onoi Y, Brand C, Christen B, Nowakowski AM, et al.
articles.population: Patients undergoing revision total knee arthroplasty between 2015 and 2024
articles.outcomes: Causes of revision failure, timing of failure, temporal trends in revision indications, patient risk factors for infection
articles.take_home: Patellar problems are the most common reason for first knee replacement revisions, but infections become increasingly common in repeated revisions. Revisions are rising significantly, with men and patients with higher BMI at greater risk of infection after knee replacement.

articles.title: Use of Zonal Fixation Therapy and Metal Augmentation for Rotating-Hinge Knee Arthroplasty in Charcot Arthropathy: A Comparative Study.
articles.authors: Zheng W, Tang J, Xiao W, Wang X, Wang J
articles.population: Patients undergoing rotating-hinge knee arthroplasty, including those with Charcot arthropathy and non-Charcot arthropathy
articles.sample_size: 51 patients (53 knees): 16 with Charcot arthropathy (18 knees) and 35 without Charcot arthropathy (35 knees)
articles.outcomes: Prosthesis survival at 60 months (94.34% overall), patient-reported outcomes, limb alignment, complications, and revision rates
articles.take_home: Using the zonal fixation method to plan rotating-hinge knee replacement is effective for severe Charcot knee arthropathy, with similar survival rates and patient outcomes compared to non-Charcot cases at five years. Metal augmentation proved helpful for managing bone defects in Charcot knees.

articles.title: Developing International Clinical Practice Guidelines: Lessons Learned in Achieving Global Consensus for the Association Research Circulation Osseous Guideline.
articles.authors: Cheng EY, Mont MA, Goodman SB, Jones LC, Cui Q, Restrepo DJ, et al.
articles.population: Patients with nontraumatic osteonecrosis of the femoral head
articles.outcomes: Lessons learned in developing international clinical practice guidelines for osteonecrosis of the femoral head
articles.take_home: This article shares what was learned when developing the first international guideline for osteonecrosis of the femoral head. The authors emphasise that successful international guidelines need to involve experts from different countries, use effective collaboration tools, consider different healthcare systems and resources, and include what patients want.

articles.title: A History of Preoperative Falls is Associated with Increased Risk of Postoperative Medical and Surgical Complications Following Total Hip Arthroplasty:A Propensity-Matched Analysis.
articles.authors: Ilyas MH, Kang H, Freeman I, Sampson WT, Mannina C, Kwon YM
articles.study_type: Propensity-matched cohort study using national database
articles.population: Adults aged 65 years and older undergoing primary total hip arthroplasty
articles.outcomes: Postoperative complications including readmission, wound disruption, acute kidney injury, sepsis, pneumonia, myocardial infarction, revision surgery, periprosthetic fracture, periprosthetic joint infection, dislocation, aseptic loosening, postoperative falls, and mortality at 90 days, one year and two years
articles.take_home: Older adults who had fallen in the year before hip replacement surgery had significantly higher rates of medical complications, infections, revision surgery, and falls after their operation compared to those without a fall history. Doctors should routinely assess fall risk before hip surgery to identify patients who might benefit from extra support and preparation.

articles.title: 30-Year Outcomes of a Cementless Total Knee Arthroplasty System in Patients Under 50 Years Old.
articles.authors: Nielsen AW, Bloebaum RD, Hofmann AA
articles.study_type: Retrospective case series with long-term follow-up
articles.population: Patients aged 50 years or younger at surgery (mean age 41 years) undergoing cementless total knee replacement
articles.sample_size: 57 patients with 75 knee replacements (27 patients and 34 knees at 30-year follow-up)
articles.outcomes: Knee Society Scores improved from 131 to 191 (P < 0.0001). Overall component survivorship: femoral component 100%, tibial baseplate 97.3%, tibial polyethylene 82.7%, metal-backed patella 94.2%. Five component failures (two tibial loosening, two infections, three patella failures). Polyethylene exchange required in 17.3% of knees. No progressive radiolucency on radiographs.
articles.take_home: This cementless knee replacement system showed excellent long-term survival over 30 years in younger patients, with most people having good knee function. The main reason for revision surgery was wear of the plastic insert, rather than loosening of the main components.

articles.title: Revision ACL reconstruction using a modified iliotibial band-allograft technique for combined ACL and lateral extra-articular procedure achieves comparable outcomes to bone-patellar tendon-bone with modified Lemaire.
articles.authors: Mas V, Giardino A, Ripoll T, Carrozzo A, Pailhe R, Courtot L, et al.
articles.study_type: Retrospective cohort study with prospectively collected data
articles.population: Adult patients aged 18 years and over undergoing revision ACL reconstruction
articles.outcomes: Primary outcome: graft retear rate. Secondary outcomes: IKDC subjective score, Lysholm score, complications, anterior laxity and pivot-shift grade
articles.take_home: Both surgical techniques had similarly low graft failure rates (around 5 per cent or less) and similar complication rates. The ITB-allograft technique produced slightly better patient-reported outcome scores and showed better results on pivot-shift testing, though the real-world significance of these differences is unclear.

articles.title: Floating fibula with associated multiligament knee injury: a case report and literature review.
articles.authors: Lorange JP, Dufour A, Chapleau J, Rouleau D, Therrien E
articles.population: Adult with high-energy knee trauma from motorcycle accident
articles.outcomes: Knee stability, range of motion (0-115 degrees flexion), ankle dorsiflexion preservation, anatomic alignment on imaging at 12 months
articles.take_home: A rare combination injury of a completely detached fibula bone with severe ligament damage to the knee was successfully treated with surgery to reconstruct the damaged ligaments and fix the fibula back in place. At 12 months, the patient had a stable knee with good movement.

articles.title: Biomechanical evaluation of fixation techniques for isolated Letenneur Type I lateral Hoffa fractures: A cadaveric model.
articles.authors: Baltaci C, Baltaci PK, Kocaoglu H, Acar HI, Uslan Y, Kalem M
articles.population: Fresh-frozen distal femurs
articles.outcomes: Ultimate load, stiffness, load at 3-mm displacement, and displacement under static and dynamic loading
articles.take_home: Adding a plate to screw fixation for this type of knee fracture made the repair stronger and more stable than using screws alone. Lateral plating worked as well as posterior plating but was technically easier.

articles.title: A prospective case series of autologous protein solution injection in knee osteoarthritis: two-year outcomes stratified by structural severity and inflammatory phenotype.
articles.authors: Satake S, Sakao K, Shimada N
articles.population: Adults with knee osteoarthritis (Kellgren-Lawrence grade 2-4)
articles.outcomes: Pain (VAS), function (KOOS Pain and ADL), treatment response rate (OMERACT-OARSI), need for knee replacement surgery within 2 years
articles.take_home: A single injection of autologous protein solution reduced pain over two years and most patients (94%) avoided knee replacement surgery during that time. The study was small and had no control group, so it cannot prove the injection caused the improvement.

articles.title: SALTO TALARIS Total Ankle Arthroplasty at a Minimum of 10 Years of Follow-up: Survival and Radiographic and Clinical Outcomes in a Series of 144 Ankles.
articles.authors: Sadoun M, Gaudot F, Bauer T, Stiglitz Y
articles.population: Patients with end-stage ankle osteoarthritis undergoing total ankle replacement
articles.sample_size: 155 patients (164 ankles) with mean follow-up of 12 years
articles.outcomes: Implant survival, revision rates, ankle movement, pain and function scores (AOFAS), number of patients reporting their ankle as a 'forgotten' joint
articles.take_home: The SALTO TALARIS ankle replacement had excellent long-term results at 10 years, with 94% of ankles remaining free from further surgery on the ankle joint. Patients reported good movement and function, with 43% reporting their replaced ankle felt normal in daily life.

articles.title: The Effect of Bariatric Surgery on Complication Risk Following Total Hip Arthroplasty in Patients with Morbid Obesity.
articles.authors: Lex JR, Ekhtiari S, Entezari B, Koucheki R, McLawhorn AS, Pincus D, et al.
articles.population: Adults undergoing primary elective total hip arthroplasty, with and without morbid obesity, and with and without prior bariatric surgery
articles.sample_size: 148,977 patients (10,287 with morbid obesity; 637 with prior bariatric surgery)
articles.outcomes: Major surgical complications within 1 year (revision arthroplasty, periprosthetic fracture, periprosthetic joint infection, dislocation); 30-day hospital readmission; length of stay
articles.take_home: Patients who had bariatric surgery before hip replacement had higher rates of complications, including infection, compared to those who did not have bariatric surgery. Hip replacement surgery should not be routinely delayed to allow for weight loss surgery.

articles.title: Transfusions are Associated with an Elevated Risk of Venous Thromboembolism and Periprosthetic Joint Infection following Revision Total Knee Arthroplasty.
articles.authors: Telang SS, Kumaran P, Lim MA, Lupu J, Cooperman WS, Lieberman JR, et al.
articles.study_type: Retrospective cohort study using national healthcare database
articles.population: Patients undergoing aseptic both-component revision total knee arthroplasty
articles.outcomes: 90-day rates of venous thromboembolism (pulmonary embolism and deep vein thrombosis) and periprosthetic joint infection
articles.take_home: Patients who received blood transfusions after revision knee replacement had higher risks of blood clots and joint infection within 90 days compared to those who did not receive transfusions. These findings suggest that hospitals should consider careful blood transfusion protocols and thresholds for this type of surgery.

articles.title: Obesity and Chronic Opioids Were Not Negatively Associated with Achieving Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services Mandated Thresholds for Hip Disability and Osteoarthritis Outcome Score for Joint Replacement Scores After Primary Total Hip Arthroplasty.
articles.authors: Buller LT, Luo TD, Deckard ER, Meding JB, Meneghini RM
articles.population: Patients undergoing primary total hip replacement
articles.outcomes: Achievement of Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services mandated Hip Disability and Osteoarthritis Outcome Score for Joint Replacement improvement thresholds of 22 points (substantial clinical benefit) and 6 points (minimal clinically important difference)
articles.take_home: Most patients improved significantly after hip replacement, with 76.8% achieving the target 22-point improvement and 94.5% achieving the smaller 6-point improvement threshold. Importantly, obesity and chronic opioid use before surgery did not prevent patients from reaching these improvement targets.

articles.title: The Most Common Organisms in Periprosthetic Joint Infection: A Survey of the International Orthopaedic Device Infection Network.
articles.authors: Graham SD, Serino J 3rd, Hietpas KT, Naufal ER, Babazadeh S, Higuera-Rueda CA, et al.
articles.study_type: Retrospective multi-centre survey of international database
articles.population: Patients with confirmed periprosthetic joint infection (hip and knee replacements)
articles.sample_size: 665 patients (311 hip, 354 knee) across four institutions in Australia, Netherlands, and USA
articles.outcomes: Most common causative organisms in periprosthetic joint infection; rates of polymicrobial, fungal, and culture-negative infections
articles.take_home: Coagulase-negative staphylococci and Staphylococcus aureus were the most common bacteria causing joint infections around hip and knee replacements across all regions studied. Rates of multiple bacteria in one infection and culture-negative cases varied significantly between countries, suggesting that antibiotic choice may need to be tailored to local patterns.

articles.title: Early postoperative changes and temporal evolution of coronal alignment following medial-closing wedge distal femoral varus osteotomy: A longitudinal radiographic study.
articles.authors: Chen YT, Tsai YC, Hong CK
articles.population: Patients with valgus knee deformity and lateral unicompartmental osteoarthritis undergoing medial closing-wedge distal femoral varus osteotomy
articles.outcomes: Weight-bearing line ratio, joint-line obliquity, joint-line convergence angle, and lateral distal femoral angle measured on standing radiographs at baseline, 3 months, 6 months, and 12 months
articles.take_home: The surgery effectively corrected knee alignment, with most changes happening within the first 6 months after surgery and then stabilising by 12 months. The alignment improvements are likely due to soft-tissue healing, how the joint distributes weight differently, and strengthening from rehabilitation.

articles.title: Evaluating generative AI responses at the 3-month timepoint following total knee arthroplasty.
articles.authors: Dickson K, Travers H, Mazzocco J, Smith EL
articles.population: Patients 3 months after total knee replacement
articles.sample_size: 4 AI models assessed; 10 orthopaedic surgeons as raters
articles.outcomes: Quality of AI responses to common patient questions about return to sport, range of motion, persistent symptoms, and scar healing, rated on a 1-4 scale by surgeons
articles.take_home: Generative AI chatbots can help answer patient questions after knee replacement, but the quality varies between platforms, with Gemini performing most consistently. Surgeon oversight remains important to ensure information is safe and accurate.

articles.title: Patellar resurfacing in total knee replacement: 20-year clinical and economic results of a large multicentre, randomised controlled trial in the UK.
articles.authors: Murray DW, Hudson J, Dakin H, MacLennan G, Little M, Gray A, et al.
articles.population: Adult patients aged 18 years and over undergoing primary total knee replacement in the UK
articles.sample_size: 1715 participants randomly assigned (861 to patellar resurfacing, 854 to no resurfacing)
articles.outcomes: Primary outcome was Oxford Knee Score; secondary outcomes included SF-12, EQ-5D-3L, quality-adjusted life-years, costs, and need for further knee surgery over 20 years
articles.take_home: There was no significant difference in knee function scores between patients who had their kneecap resurfaced during knee replacement and those who did not. However, patients who had resurfacing gained more quality-adjusted life-years and the procedure was cost-effective, with similar overall healthcare costs over 20 years.

articles.title: Triple-Taper Stems in Obese Total Hip Arthroplasty Patients: Complication and Revision Rates from a U.S. Registry.
articles.authors: Sambare TD, Royse KE, Son SJ, Fasig BH, Paxton EW, Eisemon EO, et al.
articles.study_type: Retrospective cohort study using U.S. registry data
articles.population: Obese patients undergoing cementless total hip replacement for osteoarthritis
articles.outcomes: Aseptic revision rates, periprosthetic fracture risk, and revision probability at five years, stratified by stem design type and obesity class
articles.take_home: Triple-taper femoral stems were associated with lower rates of loosening and fracture around the implant compared to single and dual-taper stems in obese patients undergoing hip replacement. This suggests the triple-taper design may provide better stability and durability in heavier patients.

articles.title: The Impact of Spinal Anesthetic Dose on Length-of-Stay in Primary Total Knee Arthroplasty.
articles.authors: Kuznetsov M, Playter KP, Travers HI, Ly SM, Hollenbeck B, Erdman J, et al.
articles.population: Patients undergoing primary total knee arthroplasty at a single hospital outpatient department
articles.outcomes: Hospital length of stay in relation to spinal anaesthetic bupivacaine dose
articles.take_home: Patients who received 1.6 millilitres or less of spinal anaesthetic had a six-hour shorter hospital stay than those who received more. Each additional 0.1 millilitres of anaesthetic was linked to about 49 minutes longer in hospital.

articles.title: Kidney Injury and Mortality Are Minimal Following Two-Stage Treatment of Periprosthetic Joint Infection.
articles.authors: Epley R, Stoops TK, Deckard ER, Buller LT, Meneghini RM
articles.population: Patients undergoing two-stage surgery for periprosthetic joint infection (infected hip and knee replacements)
articles.outcomes: Acute kidney injury incidence, kidney function markers (serum creatinine, glomerular filtration rate, blood urea nitrogen), and mortality at 90 days, one year, two years and five years
articles.take_home: Two-stage surgery for infected joint replacements caused minimal kidney injury (9.4% overall, 4.3% in patients without pre-existing kidney disease) and kidney function remained stable throughout treatment. Mortality was very low at 1.4% within one year, with deaths unrelated to the treatment.

articles.title: Postoperative rehabilitation after transtibial pullout repair of medial meniscus posterior root tears: A systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Tageldein MM, Alhamdah Y, Bouchard MD, Vivekanantha P, Murphy B, Hoshino Y, et al.
articles.population: Patients undergoing transtibial pullout repair for medial meniscus posterior root tears
articles.outcomes: International Knee Documentation Committee score, Knee injury and Osteoarthritis Outcome Score Quality of Life, KOOS Sport/Recreation, and visual analogue scale pain score
articles.take_home: Patients showed substantial improvement in knee function and pain after this type of meniscus repair surgery, but the study found no clear link between specific rehabilitation approaches and how much patients improved.

articles.title: Spinopelvic mobility and rotational morphology in patients with femoroacetabular impingement-like hip pain without classical imaging findings.
articles.authors: Kurk MB, Keskin A, Tuzel M, Albayrak K, Karslioglu B
articles.population: Patients with femoroacetabular impingement-like hip pain without classical imaging findings, and matched controls
articles.outcomes: Spinopelvic mobility measurements (pelvic tilt, sacral slope, anterior pelvic plane angle), rotational morphology (acetabular version, femoral torsion), pain scores (VAS), and hip function scores (modified Harris Hip Score, International Hip Outcome Tool-12)
articles.take_home: Patients with hip pain that looks like femoroacetabular impingement but without typical imaging findings have reduced flexibility in their pelvis and spine compared to people without hip pain. Measuring these spinopelvic movements may provide helpful additional information for patients whose symptoms and imaging findings do not match.

articles.title: Non-anatomical MPFL reconstruction and failure to address relevant pathologies are common reasons for treatment injuries following treatment for patellofemoral instability.
articles.authors: Helmbaek LM, Mikkelsen KL, Krogsgaard MR
articles.population: Patients with patellofemoral instability who filed compensation claims in Denmark
articles.outcomes: Causes of treatment injury including surgical malpractice, failure to address anatomical risk factors, nerve injuries, delayed treatment, and rare complications
articles.take_home: More than a third of complications after patellofemoral instability surgery were due to surgical errors, most commonly incorrect graft placement, and many could have been prevented with better imaging protocols and standardised treatment plans.

articles.title: Regeneration of the semitendinosus tendon and muscle after isolated semitendinosus harvest for primary anterior cruciate ligament reconstruction: A systematic review.
articles.authors: Heinz M, Lettner J, Hakam HT, Ramadanov N, Daszkiewicz M, Prill R, et al.
articles.study_type: Systematic review of primary anterior cruciate ligament reconstruction studies
articles.population: Patients undergoing primary ACL reconstruction with isolated semitendinosus autograft
articles.sample_size: 376 patients across 16 included studies (mean age 25.5 years)
articles.outcomes: Semitendinosus tendon and muscle regeneration assessed by imaging (ultrasound, MRI, CT) or histology; morphology, temporal evolution and musculoskeletal adaptations evaluated
articles.take_home: The semitendinosus tendon regrows and forms a continuous structure within the first year after harvest, but the new tendon does not restore the original anatomy and the muscle becomes permanently shortened and atrophied. Despite these changes, the tissue does regenerate well enough to maintain overall hamstring function.

articles.title: Unicompartmental knee arthroplasty is associated with greater patient preference but higher revision rates than total knee arthroplasty in the same patient: A systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.authors: Elsheikh R, Burgert N, Kievit A, de Leeuw PAJ, Hirschmann MT, Dahmen J
articles.population: Patients with a total knee replacement in one knee who had a partial knee replacement in the other knee
articles.outcomes: Range of motion, knee function scores, joint awareness, revision rates and patient preference
articles.take_home: Partial knee replacement gives better movement and patients prefer it more than total knee replacement when used in the opposite knee, but it has a higher risk of needing revision surgery. Careful patient selection and discussion of these trade-offs is important.

articles.title: Meniscal preservation in the age of biologics: toward a quantitative decision algorithm for personalized repair.
articles.authors: Rivarola H, Helito C, Collazo C, Palanconi M, Meninato M, Endara Urresta F, et al.
articles.study_type: Systematic evidence synthesis with conceptual model development and exploratory validation
articles.population: Patients with meniscal tears; validation tested on simulated cases and retrospective cohort
articles.sample_size: 50 simulated cases and 45 patients in retrospective cohort
articles.outcomes: Meniscal Preservation Score concordance with expert surgical judgment; IKDC scores at 24 months; reoperation rates
articles.take_home: Researchers developed a scoring system to help decide whether a torn meniscus should be repaired or removed based on patient age, blood supply, tear pattern, other knee damage, and activity level. Early testing showed the system agreed with expert surgeons' decisions 84-86% of the time, but the authors say more research is needed before it can be used in routine clinical practice.

articles.title: Predictors of Loss to Long-Term Follow-up After Shoulder Surgery.
articles.authors: Soule S, Smith A, Staten T, Kirkham M, Zhang C, Presson A, et al.
articles.population: Patients undergoing shoulder surgery
articles.outcomes: Completion of 2-year follow-up after shoulder surgery; predictors of loss to follow-up
articles.take_home: Younger patients were significantly less likely to complete follow-up after shoulder surgery, particularly those having instability surgery or covered by Medicaid or Workers' Compensation. Older patients and those with Medicare were more likely to stay in contact with their surgeon for the full 2 years after their operation.

articles.title: Incisional Infiltration of Liposomal Bupivacaine in Geriatric Hip Fracture Surgery: A Randomized Parallel-Group Trial.
articles.authors: Yu X, Yuan Y, Zhao S, Sun Z, Han F, Wang S, et al.
articles.population: Older adults undergoing hip fracture surgery (hemiarthroplasty or total hip arthroplasty)
articles.outcomes: Primary: resting pain at 48 hours. Secondary: pain with activity, pain medication use, sleep quality, sleep duration, nocturnal awakenings, mobilisation and hospital discharge.
articles.take_home: Adding liposomal bupivacaine to the incision did not reduce resting pain at 48 hours after hip fracture surgery in older patients. Although some secondary pain measures and sleep quality improved with the treatment, it did not help patients get up and about sooner or leave hospital faster, so routine use is not recommended.

articles.title: What hip and spinopelvic characteristics are associated with ischiofemoral impingement?
articles.authors: Leopold VJ, Telias A, Flores DV, Verhaegen J, Kerhoulas Z, Speirs A, et al.
articles.population: Young patients with nonarthritic hip pain
articles.outcomes: Prevalence of MRI-confirmed ischiofemoral impingement and associated hip, pelvic and spinal alignment characteristics
articles.take_home: Ischiofemoral impingement, found in 5.6% of patients with hip pain, is associated with specific hip bone shapes, reduced space between hip bones, and changes in spinal posture and flexibility. Both hip structure and how the spine and pelvis align together appear to play a role in causing this condition.

articles.title: A Contemporary Comparison of Modern Cementless to Cemented Femoral Stems for Treatment of Femoral Neck Fractures.
articles.authors: Chen XT, Springer BD, Borkar S, Porter SB, Spaulding A, Trousdale RT, et al.
articles.study_type: Retrospective multi-institutional cohort study with propensity matching
articles.population: Patients undergoing total hip arthroplasty or hemiarthroplasty for femoral neck fracture
articles.sample_size: 2,829 patients (598 cementless, 2,231 cemented); 346 matched pairs in main analysis
articles.outcomes: Periprosthetic fracture, bone-cement implantation syndrome (BCIS), mortality, deep vein thrombosis, and surgical site infection
articles.take_home: Cemented fixation resulted in fewer periprosthetic fractures but caused more minor bone-cement implantation syndrome symptoms than cementless fixation. However, both fixation methods were generally safe, and concerns about cementation should not prevent its use for hip fractures.

articles.title: The John Charnley Award: A Randomized Controlled Trial of Dual Mobility and Single Bearings for Patients at High Risk of Dislocation Following Primary Total Hip Arthroplasty.
articles.authors: Potluri AS, Yadav AS, Weintraub MT, DeBenedetti A, Della Valle CJ, Schwarzkopf R, et al.
articles.population: Patients at high risk of dislocation undergoing primary total hip replacement via posterior approach
articles.sample_size: 555 patients randomised (527 followed to median 23 months)
articles.outcomes: Dislocation rates, revision rates, patient-reported outcome measures at 2 years
articles.take_home: Dual-mobility bearings showed a threefold reduction in hip dislocations compared to standard bearings, but the overall dislocation rate was lower than expected so the difference was not statistically significant. Both types of bearing had similar revision rates and patient outcomes.

articles.title: Joint-specific measures improve risk adjustment in total knee arthroplasty: A machine learning approach.
articles.authors: Muller D, Gillani A, Hirschmann MT, Lazic I, Hinterwimmer F, Arber A, et al.
articles.study_type: Retrospective cohort study using machine learning
articles.population: Patients undergoing primary total knee arthroplasty
articles.sample_size: 1207 procedures at a single academic centre between 2018 and 2022
articles.outcomes: Postoperative complications (any and major) and residual pain at 1 year
articles.take_home: Adding information about the knee joint itself, such as bone damage and knee movement, helped predict which patients would have complications after knee replacement surgery. However, this extra information did not help predict which patients would have ongoing pain after surgery.

articles.title: No clinical and functional benefit after medial congruent compared to ultra congruent total knee arthroplasty at 1 year: A prospective randomized controlled study.
articles.authors: Schrednitzki D, Sina J, Lamarche A, Meissner N, Halder AM
articles.population: Patients with advanced varus knee osteoarthritis undergoing primary total knee arthroplasty
articles.sample_size: 80 patients randomised; 76 completed 12-month follow-up (38 medial congruent, 38 ultra congruent)
articles.outcomes: Primary: Knee Society Score. Secondary: range of motion, Oxford Knee Score, Forgotten Joint Score, High-Activity Arthroplasty Score, patient satisfaction, radiographic alignment
articles.take_home: Both medial congruent and ultra congruent knee implant designs produced excellent results one year after surgery with no meaningful differences between them. Either design can be used based on surgeon preference, as both achieved high patient satisfaction and similar improvements in function and pain.

articles.title: Knee arthroplasty in patients under 50 years demonstrates durable survivorship despite substantially higher lifetime revision risk than in older patients.
articles.authors: Morrison SR, Hall AJ, Lim B, Akhtar A, Walmsley P
articles.study_type: Retrospective analysis of prospectively collected registry data
articles.population: Patients aged 16-49 years undergoing primary knee arthroplasty, compared with patients aged 50 years and over
articles.sample_size: 3069 patients under 50 years (58.6% female, mean age 44); matched comparison cohort aged 50 years and over
articles.outcomes: Revision rate, time to revision, cumulative incidence of revision, lifetime revision risk, and mortality over 21-year follow-up
articles.take_home: Knee replacements in patients under 50 work well in the medium term, but younger patients face a substantially higher risk of needing revision surgery later, with an estimated lifetime revision risk of about 30% compared to older patients. Patients considering knee replacement at a young age should understand they are likely to need further surgery in the future.

articles.title: Moderate to high levels of spin in studies comparing open and arthroscopic Latarjet procedures for anterior shoulder instability: A systematic review from the LaTour Group.
articles.authors: Akrivos VS, Claizergues B, Collin P, Levy G, Valencia GV, van Rooij F, et al.
articles.population: Studies comparing open and arthroscopic Latarjet procedures for anterior shoulder instability
articles.outcomes: Prevalence and type of spin (misleading reporting, misleading interpretation, inadequate extrapolation) in the literature
articles.take_home: Most studies comparing open and arthroscopic Latarjet procedures contained misleading information or selective reporting, with moderate to high levels of bias being more common than low levels. Readers should carefully interpret these study findings and look for transparent reporting of limitations.

articles.title: Highly variable proximal tibia anatomy causes controversies for proper tibial component rotational alignment in total knee arthroplasty: A current concept review.
articles.authors: Hofmann S, Tecame A, Hirschmann M, Lustig S, Seitlinger G, Innocenti B
articles.population: Patients undergoing total knee arthroplasty for osteoarthritis
articles.outcomes: Tibial component rotational alignment techniques, patellar tracking, functional outcome and anterior knee pain
articles.take_home: There are many different ways to align the tibial component during knee replacement surgery, but no single method has been proven better than others for most patients. However, about 30% of patients have unusual tibial bone anatomy that needs to be identified and corrected during surgery for the best results.

articles.title: Distal femoral replacement using a hinged construct with postoperative posterior dislocation: a case series.
articles.authors: Littlefield CP, Biedermann BM, Weresh HR, Marchetti DC, Werner H, Lindeque B
articles.population: Patients aged 18-70 who underwent distal femoral replacement with rotating hinged prosthesis and experienced postoperative posterior dislocation
articles.outcomes: Posterior prosthetic dislocation; three patients required above-knee amputation
articles.take_home: This small case series of four men found that posterior dislocation after distal femoral replacement is uncommon but serious, with three of the four patients requiring amputation. Obese men with previous surgery on the affected leg may be at higher risk.

articles.title: Quality assessment of YouTube videos on total knee arthroplasty: a comparative evaluation of orthopedic surgeons and artificial intelligence models.
articles.authors: Bilgin E, Alparslan UB, Cakir O, Asoglu MM
articles.study_type: Quality assessment and comparative evaluation study
articles.population: YouTube videos on total knee arthroplasty
articles.outcomes: Educational quality scores using DISCERN, JAMA criteria, Global Quality Score, PEMAT, and TKA-Specific Scoring System; comparison of assessments by orthopaedic surgeons versus artificial intelligence models
articles.take_home: Most widely viewed YouTube videos about knee replacement surgery have low to moderate educational quality. Artificial intelligence tools agreed well with orthopaedic surgeons' quality assessments and could help identify better videos, though they should not be the only tool used to judge educational content.

articles.title: Soft tissue-based selection of alignment and implant strategy in total knee arthroplasty is associated with comparable patient-reported outcomes despite baseline differences.
articles.authors: Ogawa H, Nakamura Y, Ichikawa K, Ota Y, Akiyama H
articles.population: Patients undergoing total knee replacement
articles.sample_size: 158 knee replacements (84 kinematically aligned, 74 mechanically aligned)
articles.outcomes: Knee Society Score, patient satisfaction, and WOMAC scores (pain, stiffness, function) at minimum 2 years follow-up
articles.take_home: Choosing different knee replacement alignment strategies and implant types based on each patient's individual soft tissue condition and deformity led to similarly good patient outcomes. The kinematically aligned approach showed slightly better pain relief scores, but both approaches achieved comparable overall results.

articles.title: Fixation strength of anterior tibial tuberosity osteotomy in revision knee arthroplasty according to cerclage wire configuration: An experimental animal model.
articles.authors: Males J, Gomez-Aux JP, Stangl-Correa P, Stangl-Herrera W, Cantor E, Palacio-Villegas JC
articles.population: Porcine (pig) knee joints
articles.sample_size: 40 complete porcine knee joints, 10 per group
articles.outcomes: Maximum displacement and maximum load to failure of four different wire cerclage configurations for anterior tibial tuberosity osteotomy fixation
articles.take_home: In this animal study, using an angled wire configuration (inferior oblique) to hold the shinbone after revision knee surgery was stronger and moved less than the standard horizontal wire method currently used.

articles.title: Comparative Analysis of Clinically Meaningful Recovery Trajectories Following Unicompartmental Arthroplasty and Patello-Femoral Arthroplasty.
articles.authors: Singh R, Williams DL, Alexander J, Marrache M, Bedair HS, Melnic CM
articles.study_type: Retrospective comparative analysis from multi-institutional registry
articles.population: Patients with isolated compartmental knee osteoarthritis treated with partial knee arthroplasty
articles.sample_size: 2,487 patients (1,938 medial UKA, 283 lateral UKA, 256 PFA)
articles.outcomes: Time to minimal clinically important difference in patient-reported outcome measures (KOOS-PS, PROMIS Global Physical Health, PROMIS PF-10a); conversion to total knee arthroplasty; non-conversion reoperation rates
articles.take_home: All three types of partial knee replacement (medial UKA, lateral UKA, and PFA) showed similar recovery speeds and achievement of meaningful improvement within about 1-2 months. However, patients having patello-femoral arthroplasty had higher rates of needing further surgery within two years compared to the other procedures.

articles.title: Risk factors for asseptic loosening after revision total knee replacement (rTKR): a component-specific analysis of femoral and tibial reconstruction.
articles.authors: Souto CS, Pereira F, Carvalho A, Sousa R
articles.population: Patients undergoing revision total knee replacement for aseptic loosening
articles.sample_size: 215 revision total knee replacements (12 cases underwent re-revision)
articles.outcomes: Re-revision surgery rates; radiographic fixation assessed using D'Amato scoring system; analysis of augments and metaphyseal sleeves use
articles.take_home: Tibial augments were associated with higher rates of needing further revision surgery after knee replacement revision, whilst metaphyseal sleeves appeared to have lower failure rates. Achieving stable fixation and adequate reconstruction are important for long-term success.
